channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: India and US Push for Early Interim Trade Agreement as American Team Plans Delhi Visit
item.description: India and the United States are working towards the early conclusion of an interim trade agreement, with an American delegation expected to visit New Delhi soon to continue negotiations. External Affa...

item.title: Trump criticises Republican critics as Cruz and Graham warn over proposed Iran nuclear deal
item.description: President Donald Trump has publicly clashed with senior figures within his own Republican Party over a developing framework for a potential agreement with Iran. According to reports, the emerging deal...

item.title: Pep Guardiola Ends Manchester City Era With Emotional Goodbye After Aston Villa Defeat
item.description: Pep Guardiola bid farewell to Manchester City fans in an emotional atmosphere after the club lost 2-1 to Aston Villa in his final match at the Etihad Stadium. The defeat came just days after City’s Pr...

item.title: Lehohla calls for data-driven reparations and African statistical sovereignty in response to Macron’s slavery remarks
item.description: In this opinion piece, Pali Lehohla reflects on French President Emmanuel Macron’s remarks during a ceremony marking 25 years since France recognised slavery as a crime against humanity. Macron’s ques...

item.title: Piyush Goyal to hold talks with Canadian PM Mark Carney in Ottawa to advance India-Canada trade agreement
item.description: India’s Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal is on an official visit to Canada from Monday to Wednesday, during which he will meet Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney in Ottawa on the first day...

item.title: DNA research identifies additional sailors from the 1845 Franklin expedition
item.description: Researchers studying the disastrous 1845 Franklin Northwest Passage expedition have identified more crew members through advanced DNA analysis, shedding fresh light on one of Britain’s most infamous p...

item.title: NDLEA Arrests 63-Year-Old Chinese Woman at Lagos Airport Over 31kg Synthetic Cannabis
item.description: Operatives of the National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) don arrest a 63-year-old Chinese woman, Ting Hung Kiong, for allegedly attempting to smuggle synthetic cannabis known as Canadian Loud in...

item.title: Bauchi Governor Directs Improvement of Sports and Learning Facilities at Jibrin Aminu Special School
item.description: Governor Bala Abdulkadir Mohammed of Bauchi State has directed the upgrade of sports facilities at Jibrin Aminu Special School as part of ongoing efforts to improve education infrastructure and suppor...

item.title: Rare Behind-the-Scenes Publicity Photos Highlight Tom Hanks and Daryl Hannah During the Making of “Splash”
item.description: The article revisits the 1984 romantic fantasy comedy “Splash” through a collection of publicity photos featuring Tom Hanks and Daryl Hannah during production. Directed by Ron Howard, the film was a l...

item.title: Iran and US Near Agreement but Key Clauses and Nuclear Issues Still Unresolved
item.description: Iran and the United States appear close to reaching an agreement aimed at reducing tensions and ending the ongoing regional conflict, but important disagreements are still preventing the final signing...

item.title: Iranian schools continue online learning for three months amid ongoing war disruptions
item.description: Education in Iran has been significantly affected by ongoing conflict, leading to the continuation of online classes for at least three months. According to reporting by Agence France-Presse, schools ...

item.title: Auric Mining lifts Munda gold resource by 32% following strong WA Starter Pit results
item.description: Auric Mining has reported a significant upgrade to its flagship Munda gold project located near Widgiemooltha, around 82 kilometres south of Kalgoorlie in Western Australia. The company’s updated mine...

item.title: FreeBSD Laptop Experience Improves as Foundation Director Tests Daily Desktop Use
item.description: A recent discussion highlighted the experience of the FreeBSD Foundation’s executive director attempting to use FreeBSD as a full-time operating system on a modern Framework laptop. The presentation, ...

item.title: Severe thunderstorm watch in effect for southeast and central Saskatchewan regions
item.description: A severe thunderstorm watch has been issued for portions of southeast and central Saskatchewan, as meteorological conditions become increasingly favourable for the development of strong to potentially...

item.title: Asian markets rise and oil prices drop amid reports of progress in US–Iran talks
item.description: Asian stock markets mostly moved higher on Monday while oil prices fell sharply following remarks from US President Donald Trump suggesting that negotiations aimed at ending the conflict involving Ira...

item.title: B.C. activists allege abuse after Israeli interception of Gaza aid flotilla
item.description: Three activists from British Columbia who were detained after Israeli forces intercepted a Gaza-bound aid flotilla in the Mediterranean Sea have returned to Vancouver and publicly described allegation...

item.title: Gauteng struggles with rising informal settlements as authorities deploy satellite systems to monitor land invasions
item.description: Gauteng province is facing a significant and growing challenge with informal settlements, with official data revealing that there are now more than 860,000 informal structures across the province. Acc...

item.title: The Rare 1959 Cadillac Broadmoor Skyview Sightseeing Limousine and Its Space-Age Design
item.description: The 1959 Cadillac Broadmoor Skyview is a rare and highly distinctive example of mid-20th century American automotive luxury and design innovation. Built by the Superior Coach Corporation, this special...

item.title: Electric vehicle travel initiative launches across the Philippines
item.description: This article announces the launch of an electric vehicle (EV) travel initiative across the Philippines starting today, May 25, 2026. Published under ABS-CBN’s BrandNews section, the piece is identifie...

item.title: A Look at Vanessa Paradis’ Childhood and Early Rise to Fame in France
item.description: The article revisits the childhood and early career of French singer and actress Vanessa Paradis through a collection of vintage photographs from the 1970s and early 1980s. Born on December 22, 1972, ...
